stem: The perimeter of a right triangle is 60 + 20\sqrt{3} centimeters, and one of the acute angles measures 30 degrees. What is the area of the triangle, in square centimeters?
explanation: In a 30-60-90 right triangle, the sides are in the ratio 1 : sqrt(3) : 2. Let the shortest side (opposite 30 degrees) be a. Then the sides are a, a sqrt(3), and 2a. The perimeter is a + a sqrt(3) + 2a = a(3 + sqrt(3)). Setting this equal to 60 + 20 sqrt(3): a(3 + sqrt(3)) = 60 + 20 sqrt(3). Solving for a: a = (60 + 20 sqrt(3))/(3 + sqrt(3)). Rationalizing by multiplying by (3 - sqrt(3))/(3 - sqrt(3)): numerator = (60 + 20 sqrt(3))(3 - sqrt(3)) = 180 - 60 sqrt(3) + 60 sqrt(3) - 20(3) = 180 - 60 = 120. Denominator = 9 - 3 = 6. So a = 120/6 = 20. The legs are a = 20 and a sqrt(3) = 20 sqrt(3). Area = (1/2)(20)(20 sqrt(3)) = 200 sqrt(3). Verification: Perimeter = 20 + 20 sqrt(3) + 40 = 60 + 20 sqrt(3). Confirmed.

distractorRationale.A: Correctly finds a = 20 but computes area using only the short leg for both dimensions of the triangle, as if both legs equal a: area = (1/2)(20)(20) = 200
distractorRationale.B: Correctly finds a = 20 but uses the hypotenuse 2a = 40 as the second leg instead of a sqrt(3), computing area = (1/2)(20)(40) = 400
distractorRationale.C: Correct: Finds a = 20 from the perimeter equation, identifies the two legs as 20 and 20sqrt(3), and computes area = (1/2)(20)(20sqrt(3)) = 200sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.D: Correctly finds a = 20 but uses the long leg a sqrt(3) = 20 sqrt(3) for both legs, computing area = (1/2)(20 sqrt(3))(20 sqrt(3)) = (1/2)(400)(3) = 600
